mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding corporate revenue expectations and household income growth, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day SPX returns occur in less than 55% of months when PAYEMS is increasing month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held below 0.5%, persistent liquidity accommodation suppresses short-term discount rates and supports risk asset valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Sharpe ratio of long positions during triggered periods is below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month expansion in industrial production and subdued consumer price inflation indicates a non-inflationary growth acceleration, which reduces the risk of monetary policy preemption and lifts equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint z-score of INDPRO and CPIAUCSL fails to predict direction with Rank IC < 0.04, or the interaction term is insignificant (p > 0.05) in a logistic regression over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns only when the unemployment rate is elevated above 7%, because in a slack economy demand surprises are interpreted as recoveries rather than inflationary overheating.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth fails to produce positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE > 7%, or the signal's hit rate is not statistically different from the baseline hit rate when UNRATE <= 7%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 550 thousand units signal a durable construction recovery and positive wealth effects that translate into above-trend S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 45% of months when HOUST > 550, or the mean return during triggered periods does not exceed the unconditional mean by at least 1% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls exceeding 200 thousand indicates accelerating job creation that boosts consumer spending expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day SPX returns follow fewer than 60% of triggers when PAYEMS increases by more than 200k in a month, or the signal's precision is not significantly above random (binomial p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP signals expanding economic output and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day SPX returns occur in less than 55% of quarters when GDPC1 is rising, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating consumer price index signals impending monetary policy tightening that raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered periods when CPIAUCSL acceleration exceeds 3%, or the negative Rank IC is not significantly different from zero (p > 0.05).
